findings.message: New POST /items/{item_id}/archive endpoint adds a state-mutating code path with no explicit log, span, or domain metric. Only the generic ObservabilityMiddleware counters apply; the archive action itself (a write committed to the DB) is invisible in logs/traces, so failures or unexpected archives cannot be diagnosed via the correlation id.
findings.suggestion: Acquire the logger (get_logger()) and emit an info log on successful archive including item_id and the correlation id; if tracing is enabled, wrap the DB read+commit in an explicit span (e.g. tracer.start_as_current_span('archive_item')).

findings.message: The 404 error path raises HTTPException without any log line carrying the correlation id, so missing-item lookups are not observable. The diff's error branch is silent in logs and metrics (middleware only records status code at response time, not the domain reason).
findings.suggestion: Log the not-found event with structured fields (item_id, correlation id) before raising HTTPException, e.g. log.warning('archive_item_not_found', item_id=item_id).

findings.message: session.commit() is performed without an explicit log/span; any commit failure surfaces only as a 500 via the global handler with no breadcrumb identifying this code path or the affected item_id, weakening incident triage for this mutating endpoint.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the mutation+commit in a try/except that logs and re-raises (log.error('archive_item_commit_failed', item_id=item_id, exc_info=True)), and/or instrument with a tracing span around the commit.

findings.message: No SLO threshold is defined for this new mutating endpoint. default_slos() only covers global request_latency_p99_ms and error_rate_pct; a write endpoint typically warrants its own latency/error SLO (or at minimum an explicit acknowledgement that the global SLOs cover it) so alerts in provisioning.py can target archive-specific regressions.
findings.suggestion: Either confirm via comment that the global SLOs cover this endpoint, or add an endpoint-specific SLO key (e.g. archive_latency_p99_ms) in observability/slo.py and a PromQL mapping in observability/provisioning.py.

findings.message: Local `from fastapi import HTTPException` inside the function body is harmless but obscures observability review tooling that scans imports; more importantly, the function lacks any structured context (logger binding) so correlation-id propagation into the error path is not established here.
findings.suggestion: Hoist the HTTPException import to module scope and bind a request-scoped logger at the top of the handler so all branches (success + 404 + commit error) share the same correlation id.
